author: dunkin-novice
done: Added a protocol === 'capacitor:' early return in isLocalDevHost() in js/asset-config.js. The function previously matched on hostname only ('localhost' / '127.0.0.1' / '[::1]'), which collides with capacitor://localhost — Capacitor's WKWebView scheme. The collision routed Capacitor through the dev-revision branch (dev-${Date.now()}), busting the immutable CDN cache on every reload. Guarding inside isLocalDevHost means any future caller of that helper also benefits, not just getDefaultAssetRevision.
fix: Early-return false in isLocalDevHost when window.location.protocol === 'capacitor:'.
goal: Stop iOS Simulator from redownloading every card art image on every reload once the Capacitor wrap lands. Pre-emptive fix during Track 1.11 scaffold.
notes: Lands directly on main, no feature branch — surgical 4-line guard with non-controversial scope. Companion to Track 1.11 Capacitor scaffold (separate branch). Has zero runtime effect on the live site (production is https://, http://localhost is unaffected); only matters once a Capacitor wrap exists.
root_cause: isLocalDevHost identified Capacitor's WebView scheme as a dev environment because both share the 'localhost' hostname. Protocol was the discriminator we never checked.
verified: node --test passes 64/65 — the one failing test (tests/poster-debug-ci.test.js) is environmental (requires playwright in node_modules; pre-existing failure unrelated to this change). No other test references asset-config.

filed: Capacitor wrap dual-tree sync: js/vendor/supabase.js + updated js/auth.js must be synced to www/ and ios/App/App/public/ via the sync:cap workflow before next iOS build
Verify Cloudflare auto-gzips js/vendor/supabase.js — 695KB raw should compress to ~170KB on the wire; check _headers if it doesn't
Debug commit ec7efa7 remains in git history — content reverted in this patch but the commit itself is not removed
fix: Vendor @supabase/supabase-js@2.105.4 as a self-contained ESM bundle at js/vendor/supabase.js (695KB raw via esbuild). Switched the dynamic import target in js/auth.js from the esm.sh URL to ./vendor/supabase.js. Also stripped temporary debug console.log lines from js/ladder-b.js that were shipped in commit ec7efa7.
goal: Fix Safari iOS+desktop breaking all Supabase auth flows due to dynamic import of esm.sh module
notes: Rebundle command for future supabase-js updates: npx esbuild ./node_modules/@supabase/supabase-js --bundle --format=esm --outfile=js/vendor/supabase.js — note the leading ./ is required; esbuild interprets bare paths as package names. package-lock.json committed for the first time in repo history per founder decision.
root_cause: Dynamic ESM import of a remote CDN URL (esm.sh) is unreliable in Safari — the module either fails to parse, fails CORS preflight, or hangs depending on iOS/desktop version. Chrome/Firefox tolerate it; Safari does not.
symptom: On Safari iOS and Safari desktop, await import('https://esm.sh/@supabase/supabase-js@2') fails, so the client never instantiates and every authed surface silently degrades (login, reading history sync, Ladder B).

done: Added resetActiveDeck() in js/data.js (3-line export that sets module-level activeDeckId back to DEFAULT_DECK_ID). Extended js/auth.js onAuthStateChange listener to call resetActiveDeck() when _event === 'SIGNED_OUT'. Extended named import on js/auth.js:2 to include resetActiveDeck. root_cause: Module-level cache in js/data.js — `export let activeDeckId = getActiveDeckId();` runs once at module load. getActiveDeckId() correctly re-reads cleared localStorage after logout and returns 'moonmallow', but getActiveDeck() reads the cached `activeDeckId` variable, not localStorage. symptom: After SPA-style logout (no hard reload), deck-inventory re-renders and shows the previously-selected streak-locked deck (e.g. boba-oracle) as active even though the user has no session. Hard reload self-heals the bug

author: dunkin-novice
fix: Three functional edits in js/login-reward.js: (a) added setActiveDeck to the existing import from ./data.js so the function is in scope; (b) changed the popup-build deckBackUrl reference from DECKS.moonmallow.backImage to DECKS['veila-tarot'].backImage so the modal shows Veila's card back; (c) added setActiveDeck('veila-tarot') as the first line of the CTA click handler, before markSeen() and dismiss(). Also updated the file-header docstring (line 5) to reference Veila Tarot instead of Moonmallow and to note that the CTA grants the deck. canUnlockDeck('veila-tarot') already returns true once a user is signed in (deck has role: 'default' in js/data.js line 24), so setActiveDeck succeeds without further changes.
goal: Grant the veila-tarot deck on first Google sign-in. The login-reward popup was supposed to do this on its CTA, but the grant action did not exist anywhere in the codebase.
notes: The per-device flag pattern (localStorage 'meowtarot_login_reward_seen') means this fix lands only on devices that have not yet seen the popup. Devices where the previous broken CTA was already clicked carry seen=true and will never see the corrected popup unless localStorage is cleared. If that matters, a follow-up could bump the storage key (e.g. meowtarot_login_reward_seen_v2) to re-fire for everyone. Not done in this commit because it would also re-fire for users who legitimately dismissed the old popup. Flag retained for product decision.
root_cause: Two defects in js/login-reward.js: (1) the deckBackUrl computed at the popup-build site referenced DECKS.moonmallow.backImage, so the modal showed the starter deck art instead of the deck being granted; (2) the CTA click handler called only markSeen() + dismiss() — no setActiveDeck(), no grant call, nothing that would actually transfer veila-tarot to the user. Grepping js/ for grantDeck, unlockDeck, ownedDecks, or any veila reference confirmed there is no per-user ownership model in the code; the only mechanism that would land veila on the user is setActiveDeck('veila-tarot'), which was never invoked from the CTA. Detection chain via auth.js onAuthStateChange → maybeShowLoginReward was wired correctly; only the grant step inside the CTA was missing.
symptom: Users completing their first Google OAuth sign-in saw the welcome popup, clicked the CTA, the popup dismissed — and nothing else happened. Veila Tarot did not become the active deck. The popup itself also advertised the wrong deck art (Moonmallow card back instead of Veila).

goal: Stop /reading.html?mode=daily from rendering the daily card image twice on the result screen. The Phase 5 daily-result rebuild (commit 86fb5e8) added a ceremonial .panel--spread[data-spread-count='1'] inside renderDailyDetails — but startDailyReadingFlow's pre-existing .daily-motion-board (the deal-animation card stack) was being left in place after the reveal animation, so users saw both the revealed deal-animation card AND the new ceremonial spread card stacked vertically.
notes: ROOT CAUSE: After the Phase 5 daily-result rebuild, two code paths were both emitting a card-art image into the same stageRefs.stage container: (a) createDailyMotionStage in startDailyReadingFlow built .daily-motion-board with buildCardArt(card, 'hero') for the deal animation; (b) renderDailyDetails(cards, dict, stageRefs.stage) appended a new .panel--spread with buildCardArt(card, 'thumb') for the ceremonial layout. done: Authed 'Use this deck now' now writes meowtarot_pending_deck_claim instead of calling setActiveDeck() live, so the current reading + its poster stay on the deck they were drawn with; auth.js applies the pending deck on the next page load. js/deck-reward.js (CTA rewire + drop unused setActiveDeck import).
filed: future feature: restyle the on-screen reading to the new deck on 'Use this deck now' tap (currently the switch applies from the next reading)
fix: Replaced setActiveDeck(deck.id) in the authed CTA with localStorage.setItem('meowtarot_pending_deck_claim', deck.id) — the same deferral the unauth path uses. getActiveDeckId reads only meowtarot_active_deck, so the current page is unaffected; auth.js onAuthStateChange (L58-64) activates the pending deck on the next load.
goal: Stop the result page and share poster from showing different decks after an authed deck-unlock claim (BUG-016 #3)
notes: Could not exercise the full logged-in popup->reload->activate round trip (no test account); that path is code-traced. Mirror sync (www/ios) deferred — pre-existing drift (BUG-015); web serves canonical.
root_cause: Nothing pins the deck to a reading: js/reading.js renders the result once via getActiveDeckId() and share/poster.js resolves card art from the live active deck at generation time. The authed CTA's synchronous setActiveDeck() changed the live deck between those two reads, splitting result vs poster. The share payload has no deck field.
symptom: After a logged-in user tapped 'Use this deck now' on the deck-unlock popup, the result page kept the drawn deck's card art but a share poster generated afterwards rendered the newly-activated deck — mismatched decks.
verified: node --check js/deck-reward.js
getActiveDeckId reads only ACTIVE_DECK_STORAGE_KEY (data.js:117-120)

affects: daily.html / th/daily.html selection board on mobile/short viewports
author: dunkin-novice
done: Reclaimed the dead ~80px top padding on the mobile daily shell (site-header is display:none ≤768px) down to 14px, and replaced the card-slot's fixed max-width:64px with a viewport-height clamp so the 12-card grid shrinks on short screens. Continue now sits above the nav with no scroll across 600-844px. css/daily.css only.
fix: padding-top:14px via a (0,4,1) selector (body.daily-shell-active .page-shell.board-page.daily-shell) that beats styles.css without touching it; card-slot max-width: clamp(38px, calc((100dvh - 384px)/6), 64px) on the source-order-winning rule. Reverted the dead @media duplicate.
goal: Stop the daily board's Continue button from falling below the fold / behind the floating bottom nav on short phones (no-scroll requirement)
notes: clamp uses 100dvh so it also nudges very short desktop windows (caps at 64px ≥795px tall, so normal desktop unchanged). Mirror sync (www/ios) deferred — pre-existing drift (BUG-015); web serves canonical.
root_cause: (1) .daily-shell reserved padding-top: calc(--nav-height + ~16px) ≈ 80px to clear the fixed .site-header, but bottom-nav.css sets that header display:none ≤768px, so it was dead space. (2) The effective card-slot rule capped max-width at a fixed 64px, pinning the 12-card grid at ~345px tall regardless of viewport, so the stack overflowed 100dvh on short screens. The off-limits styles.css body[data-page='daily'] .page-shell.board-page (0,3,1) padding rule and a source-order-later daily.css card-slot rule were the true winners — earlier @media edits were dead.
symptom: On shorter phones (~600-720px usable) the daily selection board's Continue button was below the fold and overlapped by the fixed floating bottom nav, forcing the user to scroll to reach it.
verified: chrome-devtools-mcp on edited file localhost: 667px -> Continue clears nav by 25px (cards 47px)
844px -> full 64px cards, clears by 115px, no regression
600px -> clears with 38px cards (injection-verified)

(2) drawTrackingText now detects Thai (U+0E00–U+0E7F) and renders it as a single centred fillText run (no per-char split); EN letter-spacing path unchanged. Both fixes are shared, so they also fix TH rendering on the daily/question/celtic/full posters, not just Quick Pull.
goal: Fix Thai text rendering as tofu on share posters
root_cause: Two issues on the poster canvas. (1) The sans-serif font stacks ('Poppins'/'Space Grotesk', sans-serif) had NO Thai fallback, and IBM Plex Sans Thai (loaded by the app) was not in the canvas font preload — so Thai glyphs, especially combining vowels/tone marks, rendered as tofu boxes (e.g. the 3-card title ความรัก, subtitle). (2) drawTrackingText applied letter-spacing by splitting the string per code point (Array.from + fillText each char); Thai combining marks detached from their base consonant and rendered as standalone dotted-circle tofu (e.g. the Quick Pull 'คำตอบ' badge + eyebrows).
verified: Harness render (chrome-devtools MCP) with the real Google-Fonts <link> (IBM Plex Sans Thai + Noto Serif Thai) added to the TH harness pages so the font env matches the live site, cache-busted module import: TH Quick Pull (title/eyebrow/'คำตอบ' badge) + TH 3-card (title ความรัก, subtitle, tertiary, อดีต/ปัจจุบัน/อนาคต pills, radar axis labels การลงมือทำ/อารมณ์/ความคิด/ความมั่นคง + interpretation) all render clean — no tofu. Confirmed the earlier tofu was reproducible and is now gone. EN path unchanged. Real card art still needs an on-device pass (CORS-fallback on localhost).

author: dunkin-novice
fix: getActiveDeckId() now honors a ?deck= URL override (validated against DECKS) before reading localStorage — no persistence, no unlock gate, origin-independent. reading.js appends ?deck=<getActiveDeckId()> to the 'Open Live Full Meaning Page' button href (only that button; the SEO canonical stays clean). asset-resolver.js untouched (rule #4).
goal: The 'Open Live Full Meaning Page' link showed the card in the DEFAULT deck (moonmallow) instead of the deck the reading was drawn with
root_cause: The meaning-page link points to the canonical pages on https://www.meowtarot.com (getCanonicalCardUrl / buildCardMeaningUrl), but the app + readings run on the apex meowtarot.com (and the iOS app on capacitor://localhost). buildCardImageUrls (asset-resolver) picks the deck via getActiveDeckId(), which reads the origin-scoped localStorage 'meowtarot_active_deck'. On the cross-origin www page that key is absent, so it fell back to DEFAULT_DECK_ID — mismatching the reading's deck. (Live /cards/<slug>/ pages are rendered by per-card js/pages/<card>-card.js scripts, all of which use getCardImageUrl -> buildCardImageUrls -> getActiveDeckId, so a single fix at getActiveDeckId covers all 78 + card-page.js.)
verified: Local (chrome-devtools, cache-busted): /cards/death/?deck=boba-oracle -> hero image boba-oracle (ok); ?deck=not-a-real-deck -> moonmallow (guard); no param -> default. Reading button now carries the active deck across origins.
